author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XVIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the end, for the servant of the Lord, David himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, for the strong of hand, Christ in His Manhood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The words of this song which he spoke to the Lord on the day when the Lord delivered him out of the hands of his enemies, and of the hand of Saul; and he said, On the day when the Lord delivered him out of the hands of his enemies and of the hand of Saul:” namely, the king of the Jews, whom they had demanded for themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as “David” is said to be by interpretation, strong of hand; so “Saul” is said to be demanding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now it is well known, how that People demanded for themselves a king, and received him for their king, not according to the will of God, but according to their own will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ, then, and the Church, that is, whole Christ, the Head and the Body, saith here, “I will love Thee, O Lord, My strength” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will love Thee, O Lord, by whom I am strong.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, My stay, and My refuge, and My deliverer” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Lord, who hast stayed Me, because I sought refuge with Thee: and I sought refuge, because Thou hast delivered Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My God is My helper; and I will hope in Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My God, who hast first afforded me the help of Thy call, that I might be able to hope in Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My defender, and the horn of My salvation, and My redeemer.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My defender, because I have not leant upon Myself, lifting up as it were the horn of pride against Thee; but have found Thee a horn indeed, that is, the sure height of salvation: and that I might find it, Thou redeemedst Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With praise will I call upon the Lord, and I shall be safe from Mine enemies” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Seeking not My own but the Lord’s glory, I will call upon Him, and there shall be no means whereby the errors of ungodliness can hurt Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The pains of death,” that is, of the flesh, have “compassed Me about.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the overflowings of ungodliness have troubled Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ungodly troubles stirred up for a time, like torrents of rain which will soon subside, have come on to trouble Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The pains of hell compassed Me about” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Among those that compassed Me about to destroy Me, were pains of envy, which work death, and lead on to the hell of sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The snares of death prevented Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They prevented Me, so that they wished to hurt Me first, which shall afterwards be recompensed unto them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now they seize unto destruction such men as they have evilly persuaded by the boast of righteousness: in the name but not in the reality of which they glory against the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And in Mine oppression I called upon the Lord, and cried unto My God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He heard My voice from His holy temple” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He heard from My heart, wherein He dwelleth, My voice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And My cry in His sight entered into His ears;” and My cry, which I utter, not in the ears of men, but inwardly before Him Himself, “entered into His ears.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the earth was moved and trembled” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the Son of Man was thus glorified, sinners were moved and trembled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the foundations of the mountains were troubled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the hopes of the proud, which were in this life, were troubled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And were moved, for God was wroth with them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, that the hope of temporal goods might have now no more establishment in the hearts of men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There went up smoke in His wrath” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The tearful supplication of penitents went up, when they came to know God’s threatenings against the ungodly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And fire burneth from His face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the ardour of love after repentance burns by the knowledge of Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Coals were kindled from Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They, who were already dead, abandoned by the fire of good desire and the light of righteousness, and who remained in coldness and darkness, re-enkindled and enlightened, have come to life again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He bowed the heaven, and came down” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He humbled the just One, that He might descend to men’s infirmity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And darkness under His feet.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the ungodly, who savour of things earthly, in the darkness of their own malice, knew not Him: for the earth under His feet is as it were His footstool.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He mounted above the cherubim, and did fly” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He was exalted above the fulness of knowledge, that no man should come to Him but by love: for “love is the fulfilling of the law.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And full soon He showed to His lovers that He is incomprehensible, lest they should suppose that He is comprehended by corporeal imaginations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He flew above the wings of the winds.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that swiftness, whereby He showed Himself to be incomprehensible, is above the powers of souls, whereon as upon wings they raise themselves from earthly fears into the air of liberty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And hath made darkness His hiding place” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And hath settled the obscurity of the Sacraments, and the hidden hope in the heart of believers, where He may lie hid, and not abandon them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this darkness too, wherein “we yet walk by faith, and not by sight,” as long as “we hope for what we see not, and with patience wait for it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Round about Him is His tabernacle.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet they that believe Him turn to Him and encircle Him; for that He is in the midst of them, since He is equally the friend of all, in whom as in a tabernacle He at this time dwells.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Dark water in clouds of air.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor let any one on this account, if he understand the Scripture, imagine that he is already in that light, which will be when we shall have come out of faith into sight: for in the prophets and in all the preachers of the word of God there is obscure teaching.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In respect of the brightness in His sight” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 12): in comparison with the brightness, which is in the sight of His manifestation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His clouds have passed over.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The preachers of His word are not now bounded by the confines of Judæa, but have passed over to the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hail and coals of fire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Reproofs are figured, whereby, as by hail, the hard hearts are bruised: but if a cultivated and genial soil, that is, a godly mind, receive them, the hail’s hardness dissolves into water, that is, the terror of the lightning-charged, and as it were frozen, reproof dissolves into satisfying doctrine; and hearts kindled by the fire of love revive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All these things in His clouds have passed over to the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the Lord hath thundered from heaven” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in confidence of the Gospel the Lord hath sounded forth from the heart of the just One.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the Highest gave His voice;” that we might entertain it, and in the depth of human things, might hear things heavenly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He sent out His arrows, and scattered them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He sent out Evangelists traversing straight paths on the wings of strength, not in their own power, but His by whom they were sent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And “He scattered them,” to whom they were sent, that to some of them they should be “the savour of life unto life, to others the savour of death unto death.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He multiplied lightnings, and troubled them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He multiplied miracles, and troubled them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the fountains of water were seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the fountains of water springing up into everlasting life,” which were made in the preachers, were seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the foundations of the round world were revealed” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the Prophets, who were not understood, and upon whom was to be built the world of believers in the Lord, were revealed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “At Thy chiding, O Lord:” crying out, “The kingdom of God is come nigh unto you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “At the blasting of the breath of Thy displeasure;” saying, “Except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath sent down from on high, and hath fetched Me (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 16): by calling out of the Gentiles for an inheritance “a glorious Church, not having spot, or wrinkle.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath taken Me out of the multitude of waters.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath taken Me out of the multitude of peoples.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath delivered Me from My strongest enemies” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath delivered Me from Mine enemies, who prevailed to the afflicting and overturning of this temporal life of Mine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And from them which hate Me; for they are too strong for Me:” as long as I am under them knowing not God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have prevented Me in the day of My affliction” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have first injured Me, in the time when I am bearing a mortal and toilsome body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the Lord hath become My stay.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And since the stay of earthly pleasure was disturbed and torn up by the bitterness of misery, the Lord hath become My stay.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And hath brought Me forth into a broad place” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And since I was enduring the straits of the flesh, He brought Me forth into the spiritual breadth of faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath delivered Me, because He desired Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Before that I desired Him, He delivered Me from My most powerful enemies (who were envious of Me when I once desired Him), and from them that hated Me, because I do desire Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the Lord shall reward Me according to My righteousness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the Lord shall reward Me according to the righteousness of My good will, who first showed mercy, before that I had the good will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And according to the cleanness of My hands He will recompense Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And according to the cleanness of My deeds He will recompense Me, who hath given Me to do well by bringing Me forth into the broad place of faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because I have kept the ways of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That the breadth of good works, that are by faith, and the long-suffering of perseverance should follow after.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Nor have I walked impiously apart from My God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For all His judgments are in My sight” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For” with persevering contemplation I weigh “all His judgments,” that is, the rewards of the righteous, and the punishments of the ungodly, and the scourges of such as are to be chastened, and the trials of such as are to be proved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I have not cast out His righteousness from Me:” as they do that faint under their burden of them, and return to their own vomit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I shall be undefiled with Him, and I shall keep Myself from Mine iniquity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the Lord shall reward Me according to My righteousness (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Accordingly not only for the breadth of faith, which worketh by love; but also for the length of perseverance, will the Lord reward Me according to My righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And according to the cleanness of My hands in the sight of His eyes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not as men see, but “in the sight of His eyes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “the things that are seen are temporal; but the things that are not seen are eternal:” whereto the height of hope appertains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With the holy Thou shalt be holy” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a hidden depth also, wherein Thou art known to be holy with the holy, for that Thou makest holy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And with the harmless Thou shalt be harmless.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Thou harmest no man, but each one is bound by the bands of his own sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And with the chosen Thou shalt be chosen.” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And by him whom Thou choosest, Thou art chosen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And with the froward Thou shalt be froward.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And with the froward Thou seemest froward: for they say, “The way of the Lord is not right:” and their way is not right.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou wilt make whole the humble people” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now this seems froward to the froward, that Thou wilt make them whole that confess their sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thou wilt humble the eyes of the proud.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But them that are “ignorant of God’s righteousness, and seek to establish their own,” Thou wilt humble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For thou wilt light My candle, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For our light is not from ourselves; but “Thou wilt light my candle, O Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O my God, Thou wilt enlighten my darkness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we through our sins are darkness; but “Thou, O my God, wilt enlighten my darkness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For by Thee shall I be delivered from temptation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not by myself, but by Thee, shall I be delivered from temptation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And in my God shall I leap over the wall.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And not in myself, but in my God shall I leap over the wall, which sin has raised between men and the heavenly Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My God, His way is undefiled” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My God cometh not unto men, except they shall have purified the way of faith, whereby He may come to them; for that “His way is undefiled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The words of the Lord have been proved by fire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The words of the Lord are tried by the fire of tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He is the Protector of them that hope in Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And all that hope not in themselves, but in Him, are not consumed by that same tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For hope followeth faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For who is God, but the Lord?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 31) whom we serve.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And who God, but our God?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who is God, but the Lord? whom after good service we sons shall possess as the hoped-for inheritance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God, who hath girded me with strength” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God, who hath girded me that I might be strong, lest the loosely flowing folds of desire hinder my deeds and steps.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And hath made my way undefiled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And hath made the way of love, whereby I may come to Him, undefiled, as the way of faith is undefiled, whereby He comes to me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who hath made my feet perfect like harts’ feet” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who hath made my love perfect to surmount the thorny and dark entanglements of this world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And will set me up on high.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And will fix my aim on the heavenly habitation, that “I may be filled with all the fulness of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who teacheth my hands for battle” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who teacheth me to work for the overthrow of mine enemies, who strive to shut the kingdom of heaven against us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thou hast made mine arms as a bow of steel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And Thou hast made my earnest striving after good works unwearied.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thou hast given me the defence of my salvation, and Thy right hand hath held me up” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the favour of Thy grace hath held me up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thy discipline hath directed me to the end.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And Thy correction, not suffering me to wander from the way, hath directed me that whatsoever I do, I refer to that end, whereby I may cleave to Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And this Thy discipline, it shall teach me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And that same correction of Thine shall teach me to attain to that, whereunto it hath directed me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast enlarged my steps under me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor shall the straits of the flesh hinder me; for Thou hast enlarged my love, working in gladness even with these mortal things and members which are under me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my footsteps have not been weakened.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And either my goings, or the marks which I have imprinted for the imitation of those that follow, have not been weakened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will follow up mine enemies, and seize them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will follow up my carnal affections, and will not be seized by them, but will seize them, so that they may be consumed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I will not turn, till they fail.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And from this purpose I will not turn myself to rest, till they fail who make a tumult about me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will break them, and they shall not be able to stand” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 38): and they shall not hold out against me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall fall under my feet.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When they are cast down, I will place before me the loves whereby I walk for evermore.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thou hast girded me with strength to the war” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the loose desires of my flesh hast Thou bound up with strength, that in such a fight I may not be encumbered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast supplanted under me them that rose up against me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast caused them to be deceived, who followed upon me, that they should be brought under me, who desired to be over me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And thou hast given mine enemies the back to me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And thou hast turned mine enemies, and hast made them to be a back to me, that is, to follow me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thou hast destroyed them that hate me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But such other of them as have persisted in hatred, Thou hast destroyed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have cried out, and there was none to save them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who can save them, whom Thou wouldest not save?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To the Lord, and He did not hear them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor did they cry out to any chance one, but to the Lord: and He did not judge them worthy of being heard, who depart not from their wickedness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I will beat them as small as dust before the face of the wind” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And I will beat them small; for dry they are, receiving not the shower of God’s mercy; that borne aloft and puffed up with pride they may be hurried along from firm and unshaken hope, and as it were from the earth’s solidity and stability.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As the clay of the streets I will destroy them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In their wanton and loose course along the broad ways of perdition, which many walk, will I destroy them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou wilt deliver Me from the contradictions of the people” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt deliver Me from the contradictions of them who said, “If we send Him away, all the world will go after Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt make Me the head of the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A people whom I have not known have served Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The people of the Gentiles, whom in bodily presence I have not visited, have served Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “At the hearing of the ear they have obeyed Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have not seen Me with the eye: but, receiving my preachers, at the hearing of the ear they have obeyed Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The strange children have lied unto Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Children, not to be called Mine, but rather strange children, to whom it is rightly said, “Ye are of your father the devil,” have lied unto Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The strange children have waxen old” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The strange children, to whom for their renovation I brought the new Testament, have remained in the old man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And they have halted from their own paths.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And like those that are weak in one foot, for holding the old they have rejected the new Testament, they have become halt, even in their old Law, rather following their own traditions, than God’s.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they brought frivolous charges of unwashen hands, because such were the paths, which themselves had made and worn by long use, in wandering from the ways of God’s commands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord liveth, and blessed be my God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But to be carnally minded is death:” for “the Lord liveth, and blessed be my God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And let the God of my salvation be exalted” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And let me not think after an earthly fashion of the God of my salvation; nor look from Him for this earthly salvation, but that on high.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, who givest Me vengeance, and subduest the people under Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O God, who avengest Me by subduing the people under Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My Deliverer from My angry enemies:” the Jews crying out, “Crucify Him, Crucify Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From them that rise up against Me Thou wilt exalt Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the Jews that rise up against Me in My passion, Thou wilt exalt Me in My resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From the unjust man Thou wilt deliver Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From their unjust rule Thou wilt deliver Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For this cause will I confess to Thee among the Gentiles, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this cause shall the Gentiles confess to Thee through Me, O Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I will sing unto Thy Name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And Thou shalt be more widely known by My good deeds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Magnifying the salvation of His King” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God, who magnifieth, so as to make wonderful, the salvation, which His Son giveth to believers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And showing mercy to His Christ:” God, who showeth mercy to His Christ: “To David and to His seed for evermore:” to the Deliverer Himself strong of hand, who hath overcome this world; and to them whom, as believers in the Gospel, He hath begotten for evermore.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What things soever are spoken in this Psalm which cannot apply to the Lord Himself personally, that is to the Head of the Church, must be referred to the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whole Christ speaks here, in whom are all His members.
